channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Residents are placing their bins on road to reserve parking, 13th July
item.description:
I have been unable to find a parking place on return from picking up my grandchildren, this problem is getting worse - can you take action please. This was Lenor Close, Bexleyheath this afternoon. 13/7/26.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Lenor Close, Blendon, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Large pile of green waste dumped down the end of my house, 13th July
item.description:
This has been dumped seems someone has put a large pile of green waste dumped at the side of my property.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Penn Lane, Blendon, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.description:

Graffiti in back waterfront lesuire centre, need clean it up.

item.title: Outside of 66 Brondesbury Villas NW6, 13th July
item.description:
Drainage cover blocked by leaf debris. Please fully clear to be ready to cope with summer storm water deluges to avoid flood risk to immediate vicinity as was experienced in July 2021 flooding many basements.
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Brondesbury Villas, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dumped vehicle, 13th July
item.description:
Dumped vehicle
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Dabbling Close, Slade Green, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Road markings and pavement parking bay markings faded, 13th July
item.description:
The existing pavement parking bay markings and associated road markings have faded considerably and are now difficult to see. Repainting the markings would improve clarity for motorists and help ensure the parking bays remain clearly defined. This appears to be due to normal wear over time and would benefit from routine maintenance.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Olron Crescent, Blendon, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Flytipping, 13th July
item.description:
The area near my home on alsike road has become a magnet for dumping all sorts of rubbish from sofas to builders waste and it’s getting done more often

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Alsike Road, West Heath, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Shopping trolley, 13th July

Dumped shopping trolley on grass outside hodsoll court

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Hodsoll Court, St Mary Cray, Greater London

item.title: Overgrown bushes beside 24 Twigg Close, Erith, 13th July
item.description:

item.title: The two Sheffield bike stands outside 10 Plaistow Lane have been removed, 13th July
item.description:
Please investigate their removal. Thank you.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Plaistow Lane, Plaistow, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Restricted view, 13th July
item.description:
Hello, There is an issue if you are turning left from Saltash road into Axminster crescent. The tree outside 84 Axminster crescent has a number of branches near the ground level and they obscure the view of traffic coming up Axminster crescent. Please can these low branches be removed. If I recall correctly I highlighted the same problem a number of years ago. Thanks
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Axminster Crescent, East Wickham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Junction of Brondesbury Villas and Hazelmere Road next to bike store, 13th July
item.description:
Grate beginning to block up with leaf debris. Please look to clean out to ensure it can cope with storm water runoff. Thank you.
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Brondesbury Villas, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Road sign on pavement brokenroad sign, 13th July
item.description:
Road sign on pavement, trip hazard .

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Woolwich Common, Shooters Hill, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Cycle path blocked and dangerous, 13th July
item.description:
There is frequently a fleet of cycle vehicles parked on the cycle path on Druid Street. These bikes have: - 4 wheels - a hinged rear door the opens 180 degrees outwardly like a cupboard - take up the entirety of 1 lane of the cycle path - a tall 170cm+ box surrounding the bike with no clear glass or rear windows. Usually there is a line of them all parked one after the other and used for deliveries making it impossible to see if there is anyone in between them. These are also commercial bikes. On Saturday morning myself and my wife were cycling along Druid Street. A man came from the pavement and without warning or checking opened the rear door of the front bike missing my wife by centimetres. Again to be clear this is a 6ft solid door designed for transportation vehicles, so would have been potentially fatal if it had hit her. The man showed little remorse or caring about the event and indeed didn’t even remove both of his headphones when we approached him. And to make matters worse a parking inspector was walking by and did absolutely nothing to intervene or encourage legal parking if the vehicles. The bikes are there lined up for 100m+ everyday cause not only a nuisance but also a hazard. I love supporting small businesses especially ones who are fighting climate change, but this company needs to find safer parking. Other mitigating factors: - We were both on VOI bikes so we’re not able to go faster than 25kmph (15mph) so we’re not going at excessive speeds. - you are unable to see anyone either between the parked bikes or on the pavement beside the bikes from the cycle lane. F this had hit her she would have been severely injured using one of the “cycles” opened the side door onto the other lane of traffic almost hitting and injuring my wife. one member of staff packing up his bike had headphones in and witho

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 90, Druid Street, Old Kent Road, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Refuse disposal chute blocked, 13th July
item.description:
Peter Butler House, SE1 2BJ (East side of the building) The refuse disposal chute is blocked between the 3rd and 5th floors. The blockage has been present for some time and has not been cleared. It is now causing a strong unpleasant odour and a significant increase in flies, creating an environmental health concern. Urgent clearance is required.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): George Row, Bermondsey, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Old bed on Firs close near number 52, 13th July
item.description:
Old bed on Firs close near number 52

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Firs Close, Honor Oak, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Overhanging tree, 13th July
item.description:
Tree has a tpo but it is very overgrown. We are very concerned because lorries are reguarly hitting it and some of th ebranches now reach right to the other side of the road and are getting near the corrages. If there are any strong winds then the weight of the branch is worrying and these may come down and hurt someone
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 8, Main Road, St Paul's Cray, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Numerous holes and sinking at bus stop, 13th July
item.description:

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Hook Lane, Blackfen, Greater London
